changeSet.author: digitaltwinapp-api
changeSet.sql:
-- Add schema_version as a required field to every JSON Schema in -- transaction_schemas. -- -- Rationale: every metadata blob stored in transaction_metadata starts with -- { "schema_version": 1, ... } so the payload is self-contained. If the blob -- is forwarded to an external ledger, exported to a data lake, or retrieved -- a decade from now, the reader knows exactly which schema and display -- templates apply without any dependency on our internal tables. -- -- jsonb_set(target, path, new_value) is non-destructive for the other keys: -- the outer call adds the property definition, the inner call appends the field -- name to the existing required array.
UPDATE digitaltwinapp.transaction_schemas SET json_schema = jsonb_set( jsonb_set( json_schema, '{properties,schema_version}', '{"type": "integer", "minimum": 1}'::jsonb ), '{required}', (json_schema -> 'required') || '["schema_version"]'::jsonb ) WHERE trans_code IN ( 20026, 10027, 50005, 40003, 50003, 40005, 50002, 40002, 50006, 40006, 50004, 40004 )
changeSet.rollback: -- Remove schema_version from properties and required array on each schema. UPDATE digitaltwinapp.transaction_schemas SET json_schema = jsonb_set( jsonb_set( json_schema, '{required}', ( SELECT COALESCE(jsonb_agg(elem), '[]'::jsonb) FROM jsonb_array_elements(json_schema -> 'required') AS elem WHERE elem::text != '"schema_version"' ) ), '{properties}', (json_schema -> 'properties') - 'schema_version' ) WHERE trans_code IN ( 20026, 10027, 50005, 40003, 50003, 40005, 50002, 40002, 50006, 40006, 50004, 40004 )
